game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **マインスイーパQ プレミアム (Minesweeper Q Premium):** Minesweeper Q Premium is a classic puzzle game played on a grid of hidden squares, where players must uncover all non-mine squares without detonating any mines. In "Standard Mode," players tap a square to reveal its content; if it's a mine, the game ends, but if it's empty, it reveals a number indicating how many mines are in the 8 adjacent squares, or automatically clears a larger area if no adjacent mines exist. Players long-press a square to place a flag, marking it as a suspected mine, and can tap an already revealed number to "quick open" all surrounding unflagged squares if the number of flags matches the displayed digit. The game offers three classic difficulty levels (Beginner, Intermediate, Expert) with preset grid sizes and mine counts (e.g., 9x9 with 10 mines, 16x16 with 40 mines), alongside custom options for board dimensions and mine density. Progression is tracked via high scores for each difficulty, with the objective being to clear the board in the fastest time possible, and players can switch between "Standard" and "Quick Flag" modes by tapping an empty grid space for optimized flagging or opening.
game_info.description: Uncover all safe squares on the grid without hitting any mines! Each revealed number tells you how many mines are adjacent to that square. Use logic and deduction to flag suspected mines and clear the board. Complete the grid as quickly as possible to achieve the best time!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move cursor around the grid
Space: Reveal square / Quick-open numbered square
Z: Place/remove flag on square
Shift: Toggle between Standard and Quick Flag modes
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart (return to start screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and interaction test. Validates that cursor movement works correctly, squares can be revealed, and flags can be placed. Tests fundamental game mechanics without attempting to win.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move cursor randomly around the grid, revealing random safe-looking squares and placing flags on suspected mines. Alternate between revealing and flagging actions to test both mechanics.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The cursor should move smoothly across the grid, squares should reveal when space is pressed, and flags should toggle when Z is pressed. Position changes and game state updates should be validated.

TEST_2.test_description: Optimal win strategy test. Implements an intelligent solver that uses logical deduction to identify safe squares and mines. Tests whether the game can be won using perfect play.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Start by revealing corners which are statistically safer. Analyze revealed numbers to identify definite mines and safe squares. Use pattern recognition for common configurations. Flag all identified mines and reveal all safe squares until the board is cleared.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game should reach GAME_OVER_WIN state without hitting any mines. All non-mine squares should be revealed, and the solver should complete the board using logical deduction. Validates win condition is properly implemented.
